description_long: This object is a classic wall-mounted corded phone, reminiscent of vintage payphones commonly found in urban environments. The main body is rectangular and robust, with a prominent rotary dial on the upper front face, surrounded by a circular metallic plate. Below the dial, there are two rectangular metallic panels, likely representing the coin slot and instruction area, and a larger compartment that could be for coin collection. The handset is attached to the left side of the phone via a long, straight cord, and rests on a cradle integrated into the body. The phone is primarily black with metallic silver accents, giving it a utilitarian and durable appearance suitable for public use.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded phone with a rotary dial, metallic panels, and a side-mounted handset connected by a cord. The design is robust and utilitarian, typical of public payphones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, metallic panels, and the handset hanging on the left side.
description_view_1: Left side view showing the slim profile and the cord extending downward from the handset.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the phone and the handset resting against the side.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the handset and cord, with minimal detail on the phone body.
